_note: Per-season DEFENSIVE lines for 1994-1999 that exist NOWHERE else. UNC published no per-player defensive stats before 2000 (see PROGRESS.md), but its official RECORD BOOK's SEASON leaderboards name individual player-seasons with their totals. Each line below is a hand-picked factual entry transcribed from that book (the project's sourcing policy explicitly allows this), and each cites it. These are the only 1994-99 defensive-line seasons any source publishes, so they are also the only thing standing between the 1994-96 era wheel and a soft-locked draft. Lines are PARTIAL by nature (the leaderboards publish the record stat, not a full box score) - the same shape as Michigan's pre-1997 INT-only defenders. A second block (below, marked researched) carries per-season DT lines that only NARRATIVE sources state - Wikipedia/footballdb player pages quoting the season totals. They exist because the 1996-99 windows otherwise have no defensive tackle at all. Each carries the exact quoted sentence. Russell Davis's 1998 line was DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED: its own source says he 'played in only two games due to an ankle injury' yet credits 36 tackles - self-contradictory, so it is not used.
_page: Defensive Records (p.161): SACKS-Season and TACKLES FOR LOSSES-Season leaderboards

seasons.name: Greg Ellis
seasons.note: SACKS - SEASON, #3 all-time: 12.5 sacks for 99 yards. TFL not separately listed for this season; the sack count is a floor for TFL (every sack is a TFL), so tfl is recorded at the same value - a documented UNDERCOUNT, never an invention.

seasons.name: Greg Ellis
seasons.note: SACKS - SEASON: 9 sacks for 65 yards. tfl = sacks floor, as above.

seasons.name: Marcus Jones
seasons.note: TACKLES FOR LOSSES - SEASON, #7 all-time: 19. (ACC Defensive Player of the Year 1995.)
